mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy-rate trend is positive.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month FEDFUNDS changes have higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with flat or falling FEDFUNDS over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, restrictive short rates reduce the present value of future earnings and increase recession-risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value is above 4.5
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or have mean returns above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates that household income and spending are reinforcing each other, lifting expected corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-PAYEMS positive signal has a 20-day return hit rate below 50% and an average forward return no higher than cases where only one series is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in UNRATE greater than 0.3 percentage points signals weakening labor income and rising recession probability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the downside return spread versus non-triggered periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving production volumes and operating leverage, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends do not produce higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month INDPRO trends in at least two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend signals weakening residential investment and future consumer balance-sheet stress, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers have a nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 excess return relative to unconditional returns across the walk-forward test sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS is already high, equities face both margin pressure and limited scope for policy easing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-FEDFUNDS and rising-CPI signal has no more negative 5-day return response than rising CPI alone or high FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDPC1 growth confirms broad demand expansion and supports revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the latest quarterly growth rate is above zero.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 quarterly-growth signals fail to outperform nonpositive GDPC1-growth signals on 60-day S&P 500 forward returns after accounting for publication lags.
